description: Final Ceremony v2 — Voice-first 7-part conversation that marks the user's transition from 'working on quitting' to 'free', with journey artifacts, final recording, illusions cheat sheet, and post-ceremony dashboard

userStories.title: Database migration for ceremony v2
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ceremony v2 schema changes deployed so that v2 endpoints and features have the fields they need.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file `supabase/migrations/20260208_ceremony_v2.sql`
Migration adds `ceremony_ready_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE` column to `user_progress` table (nullable, no default) using `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`
Migration adds `ceremony_email_sent_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE` column to `user_progress` table (nullable, no default) using `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`

Migration adds `generation_status TEXT DEFAULT 'ready'` column to `ceremony_artifacts` table with CHECK constraint: `generation_status IN ('pending', 'generating', 'ready', 'failed')` using `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`
Update `types/database.types.ts` to include `ceremony_ready_at`, `ceremony_email_sent_at` in UserProgress type and `generation_status` in CeremonyArtifact type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Existing tables: user_progress has program_status, ceremony_completed_at, ceremony_skipped_final_dose. ceremony_artifacts has id, user_id, artifact_type, content_text, content_json, audio_path, audio_duration_ms, included_moment_ids, ceremony_completed_at, created_at, updated_at. All new columns are nullable with defaults so existing data is unaffected. Check existing types file for the exact type naming pattern.

userStories.title: Ceremony layout (immersive mode) and exit dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want the ceremony to be immersive with no header or navigation so that I stay focused on the experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `layouts/ceremony.vue` — minimal layout that renders only `<main><slot /></main>` with full-viewport styling and no AppHeader or navigation
Create `components/CeremonyExitDialog.vue` — dialog with message 'Leave ceremony? Your progress won't be saved.' and two buttons: 'Leave' and 'Stay'
CeremonyExitDialog accepts `open` boolean prop controlling visibility
CeremonyExitDialog emits `@leave` when Leave button clicked and `@stay` when Stay button clicked
CeremonyExitDialog uses `role='alertdialog'`, `aria-modal='true'`, and descriptive `aria-labelledby`
Stay button receives default focus when dialog opens
Dialog is focus-trapped when open (Tab/Shift+Tab cycle within dialog buttons only)
In `pages/ceremony.vue`, add `definePageMeta({ layout: 'ceremony' })` and a document-level Escape keydown listener that shows the exit dialog
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Existing layout: layouts/default.vue includes AppHeader. The ceremony layout deliberately omits it. For focus trap, use onKeydown to intercept Tab and cycle between the two buttons. The ceremony page already exists at pages/ceremony.vue — update it in-place. Use glass card styling and brand tokens for the dialog.

userStories.title: Ceremony system prompt builder
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ceremony-specific system prompt that defines the 7-part structure, token protocol, and user context injection so that the AI can guide the ceremony conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `server/utils/prompts/ceremony-prompt.ts` with a `buildCeremonySystemPrompt(context)` export function

Function accepts context object with: userStory (origin, triggers, stakes), momentsByType (rationalizations, insights, breakthroughs, observations as CapturedMoment arrays), illusionsCompleted (string array of 5 keys), productType (string or null)

Prompt defines all 7 ceremony parts (Reflective Journey, Illusions Recap, Why Check, Are You Ready, Final Dose/Disposal, Final Recording, Celebration) with each part's objective, emotional arc, example dialogue, and exit criteria for when to advance
Prompt includes Pre-Part 5 quit check: AI asks if user already stopped using nicotine, routes to Part 5A (symbolic disposal) if yes, Part 5B (final dose) if no

Prompt includes token protocol: emit `[JOURNEY_GENERATE]` when entering Part 6 before recording invitation, emit `[RECORDING_PROMPT]` at end of recording invitation message, emit `[SESSION_COMPLETE]` at end of Part 7 closing
Prompt instructs AI to weave user's captured moments into Part 1 narrative and use rationalization moments for Part 5 contrast (with fallback to general framing if none available)
Prompt uses specific product type when provided, falls back to 'nicotine products' when null
Prompt instructs Part 3 external motivation handling: validate but gently redirect to internal motivation
Prompt instructs Part 4 'not ready' handling: explore hesitation, offer graceful exit if unresolved (AI says ceremony will be waiting)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Look at existing prompt builders in `server/utils/prompts/` for the pattern (function signature, how they're imported). The ceremony prompt is the longest prompt in the app. Reference ceremony-spec.md §The 7-Part Ceremony Flow for exact script text and emotional arcs for each part. The LLM decides when to advance between parts based on conversational cues — no server-side state machine. The prompt should instruct the AI to be guided but flexible: follow the user's energy within each part but steer back when the beat is complete.

userStories.title: /api/chat ceremony integration with token detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/chat to handle ceremony-specific behavior so that the ceremony conversation uses the right system prompt and triggers journey generation.
acceptanceCriteria: When `/api/chat` receives `sessionType='ceremony'`, it calls `buildCeremonySystemPrompt()` to generate the system prompt instead of the default session prompt
Ceremony system prompt is populated with user context: user_story data, captured_moments grouped by type, illusions completed list, and product_type from user_intake
When the AI response SSE stream contains `[JOURNEY_GENERATE]`, the server triggers background journey artifact generation (fire-and-forget Promise that does not block the SSE response)
Tokens `[RECORDING_PROMPT]`, `[JOURNEY_GENERATE]`, and `[SESSION_COMPLETE]` are passed through in the SSE stream for client-side detection
Ceremony conversations are stored with `session_type='ceremony'` in the conversations table (consistent with existing session type storage)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: `server/api/chat.post.ts`. Add a ceremony-specific branch alongside existing session type handling. Look for where sessionType is checked and system prompts are selected. For journey generation trigger: scan the accumulated response text for [JOURNEY_GENERATE] and call the generation function (from US-005). If the generation function doesn't exist yet, create the ceremony_artifacts row with status='pending' as a placeholder. User context data can be fetched inline or received from the client (check how core session prompts get their context).

userStories.title: Background journey artifact generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want journey artifacts generated in background during Parts 6-7 so that they're ready when the user reaches the post-ceremony dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or refactor a reusable `generateJourneyArtifact(userId, supabase)` function in `server/utils/ceremony/generate-journey.ts`
Function creates a `ceremony_artifacts` row with `artifact_type='reflective_journey'` and `generation_status='pending'`
Function queries the user's captured moments and user story to build journey narrative context
Function calls the LLM to generate personalized narrative with segments (narration segments and user_moment segments with moment references)

On LLM success, populates `content_json` with JourneyArtifactData structure: `{ segments: [{ id, type: 'narration'|'user_moment', text, transcript, duration_ms?, moment_id?, audio_generated: false }] }` and sets `generation_status='ready'`
On LLM failure, sets `generation_status='failed'` and logs error with userId and error message
TTS audio generation per segment runs in background AFTER metadata is saved (does not block 'ready' status); each segment's `audio_generated` flag updated to true as TTS completes
Create `GET /api/ceremony/journey` endpoint that returns `{ journey, status }` for the authenticated user (status is the generation_status value)
Journey endpoint returns 404 if no reflective_journey artifact exists for the user
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Existing file: `server/api/ceremony/generate-journey.post.ts` — extract generation logic into a reusable function callable from both the endpoint (retry from dashboard) and the /api/chat trigger (background during Parts 6-7). TTS uses existing `server/utils/tts/` infrastructure. Look at the existing generate-journey implementation for the LLM call pattern and moment selection logic.

userStories.title: Ceremony complete endpoint v2 with cheat sheet generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ceremony/complete endpoint updated for v2 and cheat sheet generated at completion so that the conversation-based ceremony can finalize properly.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/ceremony/complete` requires `conversation_id` string in request body
`final_recording_path` is optional in request body (ceremony can complete without a recording)
On success, sets `user_progress.program_status` to `'completed'` and `user_progress.ceremony_completed_at` to current timestamp

Response format: `{ status: 'completed', ceremony_completed_at: string, journey_artifact_status: 'ready' | 'generating' | 'pending' }` where journey_artifact_status reflects the reflective_journey artifact's generation_status
Returns 400 error if ceremony is already completed (program_status already 'completed')
Generates illusions cheat sheet: creates `ceremony_artifacts` row with `artifact_type='illusions_cheat_sheet'`, `generation_status='ready'`
Cheat sheet `content_json` contains all 5 illusions (stress, pleasure, willpower, focus, identity) each with `illusionKey`, `name`, `illusion` (what they believed), `truth` (the reframe)

For each illusion, if user has a captured insight moment (from `user_story.{illusionKey}_key_insight_id`), include `userInsight` text and `insightMomentId`; otherwise omit the `userInsight` field entirely
Cheat sheet generation failure is logged but does NOT block ceremony completion
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: `server/api/ceremony/complete.post.ts` — modify in-place. Remove `already_quit` parameter (AI handles via Pre-Part 5). Remove requirement for artifacts to exist before completion. Add conversation_id as required. The 5 illusion keys and their names/truths are static content — hardcode or reference a config. Query `user_story` table for `{illusionKey}_key_insight_id` fields, then query `captured_moments` by those IDs for insight text. CheatSheetData interface: { entries: Array<{ illusionKey, name, illusion, truth, userInsight?, insightMomentId? }>, generatedAt: string }.

userStories.title: SessionView ceremony token detection and pause/resume
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SessionView to detect ceremony tokens and support pause/resume so that the ceremony page can orchestrate the recording and completion flows.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionView emits new `@recording-prompt` event when `[RECORDING_PROMPT]` is detected in an AI response message (same detection pattern as existing `[SESSION_COMPLETE]`)
SessionView exposes `pause()` method via `defineExpose` that sets an internal `isPaused` ref to true, disabling the mic button and text input
SessionView exposes `resume()` method via `defineExpose` that sets `isPaused` to false, re-enabling mic button and text input
When paused, mic tap and text submit are no-ops (user cannot send messages)
`[RECORDING_PROMPT]` and `[JOURNEY_GENERATE]` are stripped from displayed message text in the transcript (same stripping pattern as `[SESSION_COMPLETE]`)
Update `server/utils/tts/sanitize.ts` to strip `[RECORDING_PROMPT]` and `[JOURNEY_GENERATE]` tokens from TTS input text
Existing `[SESSION_COMPLETE]` detection, stripping, and event emission is unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: `components/voice/SessionView.vue`. Look for the existing [SESSION_COMPLETE] detection — it's likely a watch on messages or the SSE stream handler. Add [RECORDING_PROMPT] detection using the same pattern. For pause/resume: add `isPaused` ref, bind it to MicButton's disabled prop and the text input's disabled attribute. Token stripping in display: find where [SESSION_COMPLETE] is stripped from message text and add the new tokens. TTS sanitize: `server/utils/tts/sanitize.ts` — add to the regex or string replacement list.

userStories.title: CeremonyRecordingInline component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to record a message to my future self inline within the ceremony conversation so that the emotional flow isn't broken.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `components/CeremonyRecordingInline.vue` with recording UI extracted from v1 ceremony recording code

Component has 5 visual states: idle (mic button + 'Tap when you're ready'), recording (pulsing mic + elapsed time counter), preview (playback controls + 'Keep this recording' + 'Try again'), saving (upload indicator), saved (confirmation message)
Tapping mic in idle starts recording; tapping in recording stops and transitions to preview
'Try again' in preview discards recording and returns to idle (unlimited re-records)
'Keep this recording' triggers upload to `/api/ceremony/save-final-recording` and emits `@audio-saved(path)` on success
'or type your message' link switches to a text input; submitting emits `@text-saved(text)`
Component emits `@error(msg)` on unrecoverable errors
Recording auto-stops at 5 minutes (300s) — timer visible during recording
Client-side blob size check: if > 10MB, show error prompting shorter re-record

Upload retries up to 3 times on failure; after 3 failures, saves blob as base64 to localStorage key `'unhooked:pending-ceremony-upload'` (schema: { userId, blobDataUrl, timestamp, retryCount: 3 }) and emits `@audio-saved` so ceremony continues
If MediaRecorder throws (e.g., device storage full), catch and switch to text input mode automatically
Slide-in/out animation on appear/disappear (respects `prefers-reduced-motion`)
ARIA labels: 'Ready to record' (idle), 'Recording in progress' (recording), 'Recording saved' (saved)
Update `server/api/ceremony/save-final-recording.post.ts` to validate MIME type: accept only `audio/webm` and `audio/ogg`, reject others with 400
Update save-final-recording to validate file size server-side: reject uploads larger than 10MB with 400
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Extract recording logic from v1 `pages/ceremony.vue` — look for existing MediaRecorder usage, audio recording state machine, upload to save-final-recording. The existing `useAudioRecorder` composable may be reusable. Lazy-load this component via `defineAsyncComponent` when used in ceremony.vue. For the localStorage pending upload, the schema must match what US-017 (upload retry) expects. Use glass card styling and brand tokens.

userStories.title: useCeremony() orchestrator composable
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a composable that orchestrates the full ceremony lifecycle so that ceremony.vue stays manageable and the logic is testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `composables/useCeremony.ts` exporting the `useCeremony()` composable
Exposes reactive `ceremonyPhase` ref: 'pre-ceremony' | 'conversation' | 'recording' | 'completing' | 'transitioning' | 'error'
Exposes refs: `conversationId`, `isRecording`, `isCompleting`, `isTransitioning`, `showExitDialog`, `error` (string|null), `ariaAnnouncement` (string)

`startCeremony()` calls `GET /api/ceremony/prepare`, validates `ready===true`, stores conversationId (or active_conversation_id from response), and transitions to 'conversation' phase; transitions to 'error' if prepare fails or ready is false
`handleRecordingPrompt()` transitions to 'recording' phase and sets `isRecording=true`
`handleRecordingSaved(path?)` and `handleTextSaved(text)` transition back to 'conversation' phase with `isRecording=false`

`handleSessionComplete()` transitions to 'completing', calls `POST /api/ceremony/complete` with conversationId and optional finalRecordingPath, retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s)
On completion success, transitions to 'transitioning', sets `ariaAnnouncement` to 'Transitioning to your dashboard in a moment.'
After 5 seconds in 'transitioning' phase, calls `navigateTo('/dashboard')`; if `prefers-reduced-motion` is active, navigates immediately (no delay)
On completion failure after all retries, transitions to 'error' with error message
`handleEscapeKey()` toggles `showExitDialog`
`handleLeave()` navigates to `/dashboard`; `handleStay()` sets `showExitDialog=false`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This composable orchestrates — it does NOT own conversation state (SessionView does) or recording state (CeremonyRecordingInline does). It manages the ceremony lifecycle and coordinates between them. Use `$fetch` for API calls. Use `navigateTo` for navigation. For prefers-reduced-motion: `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches`. The prepare endpoint returns ceremony context + optional active_conversation_id for concurrent tab handling.

userStories.title: Ceremony page — pre-ceremony screen and route guards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a pre-ceremony screen before starting and be redirected if I'm not eligible or already completed so that the ceremony entry feels intentional.
acceptanceCriteria: Ceremony page uses `layout: 'ceremony'` (immersive, no header) via definePageMeta
On page load, fetch user progress to check `program_status`
If `program_status` is `'completed'`, redirect to `/dashboard` immediately with no flash of ceremony content
If `program_status` is NOT `'ceremony_ready'`, redirect to `/dashboard` immediately
If `program_status` is `'ceremony_ready'`, show the pre-ceremony screen

Pre-ceremony screen shows: 'YOUR FINAL SESSION' eyebrow heading, 'Set aside 15 minutes.' and 'Find a quiet space.' instructions, product-specific text 'If you still have your [product] around, have it nearby' (falls back to 'nicotine products' when product type unknown), 'This is a moment worth being present for.' closing, and a 'Begin Ceremony' primary CTA button
Pre-ceremony screen uses glass card styling with `animate-fade-in-up` entrance animation
Remove v1 ceremony step wizard code (intro toggle, generating step, journey player step, cheatsheet step, complete step) — this page will be rebuilt around the voice conversation in the next story
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: `pages/ceremony.vue` — refactor in-place. The v1 page has a multi-step wizard with steps like 'intro', 'generating', 'journey', 'recording', 'cheatsheet', 'complete'. Strip those out and replace with the pre-ceremony screen for now. Product type can be fetched from user intake data or the ceremony/prepare response. For redirects, use `navigateTo('/dashboard', { replace: true })` in an onMounted guard or equivalent.

userStories.title: Ceremony page — voice conversation, recording, and completion wiring
userStories.description: As a user, I want the ceremony to be a continuous voice conversation with inline recording and auto-transition so that it feels like a natural coaching session.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Begin Ceremony' calls `useCeremony().startCeremony()` and transitions to voice conversation mode showing SessionView with `sessionType='ceremony'`
When SessionView emits `@recording-prompt`, the conversation pauses (SessionView.pause()), and CeremonyRecordingInline slides in inline within the conversation view
CeremonyRecordingInline is lazy-loaded via `defineAsyncComponent` (only loaded when Part 6 is reached)
When CeremonyRecordingInline emits `@audio-saved` or `@text-saved`, it slides away and SessionView resumes via SessionView.resume()
When SessionView emits `@session-complete`, calls `useCeremony().handleSessionComplete()` triggering the completion + auto-transition flow
During 'transitioning' phase, ceremony container fades out before dashboard navigation (respects `prefers-reduced-motion` — skip fade if set)
An inline `aria-live='polite'` region is present and displays the `ariaAnnouncement` from useCeremony()
CeremonyExitDialog wired to `showExitDialog` from useCeremony() — Leave navigates to dashboard, Stay dismisses dialog
Error phase shows message 'Something went wrong. Your ceremony will be ready when you come back.' with 'Return to Dashboard' button
If user exits mid-ceremony and returns, they see the pre-ceremony screen fresh (no persisted ceremony state)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: `pages/ceremony.vue` — builds on US-010. The page is state-driven by useCeremony().ceremonyPhase: 'pre-ceremony' shows the screen from US-010, 'conversation' shows SessionView, 'recording' shows SessionView (paused) + CeremonyRecordingInline overlay, 'completing'/'transitioning' shows fade-out, 'error' shows error message. Use template ref on SessionView to call pause()/resume(). The @session-complete event fires when [SESSION_COMPLETE] token is detected in SessionView.

userStories.title: Ceremony nudge email
userStories.description: As a user who reached ceremony eligibility but hasn't started within 24 hours, I want to receive a gentle nudge email reminding me to complete my ceremony.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `server/utils/email/ceremony-email-sender.ts` with exported `processCeremonyEmails(supabase)` function
Function queries users WHERE `program_status='ceremony_ready'` AND `ceremony_ready_at <= now() - 24 hours` AND `ceremony_email_sent_at IS NULL` AND `ceremony_completed_at IS NULL`
For each eligible user, fetches product type from `user_intake.products_used`
Email subject: 'Your final session is ready'
Email body references user's specific product (e.g., 'Have your vape nearby') when known; falls back to 'nicotine products'
Email CTA button links to dashboard URL with no embedded auth token
After successful send, sets `user_progress.ceremony_email_sent_at = now()`
Skips users who already have `ceremony_email_sent_at` set (no duplicates)
Skips users who have already completed ceremony
Email sent via Resend (same pattern as existing check-in-sender.ts)
Integrate into `server/api/cron/check-ins.get.ts` — call `processCeremonyEmails()` alongside existing `processScheduledCheckIns()`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern in `server/utils/email/check-in-sender.ts` for: Resend client usage, inline HTML email template, error handling, return type. The cron endpoint at `server/api/cron/check-ins.get.ts` already runs every 5 min via GitHub Actions. Just add the ceremony email call after check-in processing. Note: ceremony_ready_at is set when program_status transitions to ceremony_ready (see US-013 for that logic).

userStories.title: Final core session tease
userStories.description: As a user completing Identity Layer 3 (the final core session), I want a natural tease about the ceremony so that I feel excited about the next step.
acceptanceCriteria: When Identity Layer 3 session completes, the AI's closing message includes a ceremony tease instruction in its system prompt (e.g., instruct AI to say 'There's one more conversation ahead. Your final session. It'll be waiting for you when you're ready.')
SessionCompleteCard component accepts an optional `ceremonyTease` boolean prop
When `ceremonyTease` is true, card shows 'All five illusions dismantled. Your final ceremony is ready.' instead of normal completion text
When `ceremonyTease` is true, only 'Return to Dashboard' CTA is shown (no other CTAs like 'Next Session')
In the session page, detect when the completing session is Identity Layer 3 and pass `ceremonyTease=true` to SessionCompleteCard
When Identity Layer 3 session completes on the server, set `user_progress.ceremony_ready_at = now()` alongside the `program_status = 'ceremony_ready'` transition
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to modify: system prompt for identity illusion layer 3 (add ceremony tease instruction), `components/SessionCompleteCard.vue` (add ceremonyTease prop with conditional rendering), the session page that renders SessionCompleteCard (detect identity layer 3), and the session completion server logic (set ceremony_ready_at). Identity Layer 3 is illusion='identity', layer=3. The ceremony_ready_at timestamp is critical for the 24h email nudge (US-012).

userStories.title: Post-ceremony dashboard — ceremony CTA and artifact cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to show a ceremony CTA when eligible and my artifacts after completion so that I can start or revisit my ceremony experience.
acceptanceCriteria: When `program_status === 'ceremony_ready'`, dashboard shows ceremony CTA card with text: 'You've seen through all five illusions. You're ready. Your final session—the ceremony—is available now.' and a 'Begin Ceremony Now' button linking to `/ceremony`
Ceremony CTA card is the primary focus of the dashboard when in ceremony_ready state (no other session CTAs competing)
When `program_status === 'completed'`, the pre-ceremony progress view is fully replaced with post-ceremony layout
Post-ceremony layout shows 'YOU'RE FREE' heading with completion date formatted nicely
Post-ceremony shows 'YOUR JOURNEY' card with play button that links to journey playback page
Post-ceremony shows 'YOUR MESSAGE' card — audio player if voice recording exists, or displayed text if typed message
Post-ceremony shows 'ILLUSIONS CHEAT SHEET' card linking to cheat sheet page
Post-ceremony shows 'Need a boost? [Talk to me]' CTA for reinforcement support
Post-ceremony shows the 5 illusion reinforcement items with '[Reinforce]' buttons
Artifact data fetched from ceremony API endpoints (journey, final-recording)
All cards use glass card styling with brand design tokens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: `pages/dashboard.vue`. The dashboard already has some post-ceremony rendering from v1 — update to match v2 layout. Ceremony CTA only appears for ceremony_ready state. Post-ceremony fully replaces the progress/session view. For 'Your Message': check if artifact has audio_path (show audio player) or content_text (show text). The journey, final-recording, and cheat sheet data come from their respective API endpoints.

userStories.title: Post-ceremony dashboard — journey loading states and polling
userStories.description: As a user who just completed the ceremony, I want to see loading progress for my journey artifact so that I know it's being prepared.
acceptanceCriteria: When journey artifact status is 'pending' or 'generating', show a skeleton card with 'Preparing your journey...' text instead of the playable journey card
Skeleton card uses glass card styling with a shimmer/loading animation
Dashboard polls `GET /api/ceremony/journey` every 3 seconds while status is 'pending' or 'generating'
Polling stops automatically when status changes to 'ready' or 'failed'
Polling stops on component unmount (cleanup interval)
When status becomes 'ready', skeleton transitions to the playable journey card
When status is 'failed', show an error state with 'Retry' button
Clicking 'Retry' calls `POST /api/ceremony/generate-journey` to restart generation, then resumes polling with skeleton
Other dashboard cards (Message, Cheat Sheet, Reinforcement) render normally regardless of journey status
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: `pages/dashboard.vue` — extends US-014's post-ceremony section. Use setInterval for polling with cleanup in onUnmounted. The skeleton card should match journey card dimensions to prevent layout shift. Poll the GET /api/ceremony/journey endpoint (created in US-005). For retry, POST to generate-journey endpoint to reset status and re-run generation.

userStories.title: Cheat sheet page — conditional user insights display
userStories.description: As a user who completed the ceremony, I want the illusions cheat sheet to show my personal insights alongside each illusion so I have a meaningful quick reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Cheat sheet page displays all 5 illusions as a vertical scroll list of glass cards
Each card shows: illusion name as heading, 'The Illusion' section with what they used to believe, 'The Truth' section with the reframe
For illusions where the user has a captured insight, a 'Your Insight' section appears with the user's quote text
For illusions without a captured insight, the 'Your Insight' section is NOT rendered (card is simply shorter — no empty state or placeholder)
All 5 illusions always display (stress, pleasure, willpower, focus, identity) regardless of insight availability
Cheat sheet data fetched from ceremony_artifacts API where artifact_type is 'illusions_cheat_sheet'
Cards use glass card styling with brand design tokens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Check for existing cheat sheet page (might be at pages/cheat-sheet.vue or similar). If it exists, update to render from the content_json CheatSheetData structure generated by US-006. Each entry has: illusionKey, name, illusion, truth, and optionally userInsight + insightMomentId. Conditionally render 'Your Insight' based on presence of userInsight field (not null check — the field is omitted entirely when absent).

userStories.title: localStorage upload retry queue
userStories.description: As a user whose recording upload failed during the ceremony, I want the upload retried automatically when I return so that my recording isn't lost.
acceptanceCriteria: On ceremony page mount and dashboard page mount, check localStorage key `'unhooked:pending-ceremony-upload'` for pending upload data
If pending upload found, convert base64 data URL back to Blob and attempt upload to `/api/ceremony/save-final-recording`
On successful upload, remove the pending upload entry from localStorage
On failed upload, increment `retryCount` in the stored object and save back to localStorage
After 10 total retry attempts (retryCount >= 10), remove the pending upload from localStorage (give up gracefully — no error shown to user)
Retry runs silently in the background with no UI indicators
PendingUpload localStorage schema matches what CeremonyRecordingInline writes: `{ userId: string, blobDataUrl: string, timestamp: string, retryCount: number }`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Can be a utility function or small composable (e.g., `usePendingUpload()`). Call in onMounted of both `pages/ceremony.vue` and `pages/dashboard.vue`. The localStorage key and schema must match what CeremonyRecordingInline writes in US-008 when upload fails 3 times. Use $fetch for the upload. Convert base64 data URL to Blob via fetch(dataUrl).then(r => r.blob()) or manual parsing.
